question.text: The minimum investment by employees or directors of the AIF or its Manager is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** While the general minimum investment is INR 1 crore, employees or directors of the AIF or its Manager have a reduced minimum of INR 25 lakh. This allows key personnel to invest in the fund they manage at a lower threshold. **The Trap:** This relaxation applies only to employees/directors of the AIF or its Manager — not to any related parties or family members. The INR 1 crore minimum applies to all other investors. **Cross-Exam Context:** Differential investment requirements for different categories of investors are tested in XIX-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Employee investment in the fund is a positive signal — it shows the team believes in the fund's strategy. When presenting funds to investors, mention if key team members have personally invested (beyond mandatory sponsor commitment).
